back_translation: On the day Moses finished setting up the tabernacle, he anointed and consecrated it and all its furnishings, and the altar and all its utensils; he anointed them and consecrated them.
And the leaders of Israel, the heads of the houses of their fathers, presented an offering. These were the leaders of the tribes, those who stood over those counted.
And they brought their offering before the LORD: six covered carts and twelve oxen — a cart from every two leaders and an ox from each — and they presented them before the tabernacle.
And the LORD said to Moses, saying:
'Accept these from them, and they shall be used to do the work of the Tent of Meeting; and you shall give them to the Levites, each man according to the word of his service.'
So Moses took the carts and the oxen and gave them to the Levites.
He gave two carts and four oxen to the sons of Gershon, according to the word of their service.
And four carts and eight oxen he gave to the sons of Merari, according to the word of their service, under the hand of Ithamar son of Aaron the priest.
But to the sons of Kohath he did not give, because the service of the holy was upon them; on the shoulder they shall carry.
And the leaders presented the dedication of the altar on the day of its anointing; and the leaders presented their offering before the altar.
And the LORD said to Moses, 'One leader per day, one leader per day, they shall present their offering for the dedication of the altar.'
And the one offering on the first day his offering was Nahshon son of Amminadab for the tribe of Judah.
His offering was one silver platter, one hundred thirty in weight; one silver bowl, seventy shekels according to the sanctuary shekel; both filled with fine flour mixed with oil as a grain offering;
one dish, ten, gold, filled with incense;
one bull, son of the herd, one ram, one lamb son of its year, for a burnt offering;
one male goat of the goats for a sin offering;
and for the sacrifice of peace offerings two oxen, five rams, five male goats, five lambs sons of a year. This was the offering of Nahshon son of Amminadab.
On the second day Nethanel son of Zuar, the leader of the sons of Issachar, presented.
His offering was one silver platter, one hundred thirty in weight; one silver bowl, seventy shekels according to the sanctuary shekel; both filled with fine flour mixed with oil as a grain offering;
one dish, ten, gold, filled with incense;
one bull, son of the herd, one ram, one lamb son of its year, for a burnt offering;
one male goat of the goats for a sin offering;
and for the sacrifice of peace offerings two oxen, five rams, five male goats, five lambs sons of a year. This was the offering of Nethanel son of Zuar.
On the third day Eliab son of Helon, the leader of the sons of Zebulun, presented.
His offering was one silver platter, one hundred thirty in weight; one silver bowl, seventy shekels according to the sanctuary shekel; both filled with fine flour mixed with oil as a grain offering;
one dish, ten, gold, filled with incense;
one bull, son of the herd, one ram, one lamb son of its year, for a burnt offering;
one male goat of the goats for a sin offering;
and for the sacrifice of peace offerings two oxen, five rams, five male goats, five lambs sons of a year. This was the offering of Eliab son of Helon.
On the fourth day Elizur son of Shedeur, the leader of the sons of Reuben, presented.
His offering was one silver platter, one hundred thirty in weight; one silver bowl, seventy shekels according to the sanctuary shekel; both filled with fine flour mixed with oil as a grain offering;
one dish, ten, gold, filled with incense;
one bull, son of the herd, one ram, one lamb son of its year, for a burnt offering;
one male goat of the goats for a sin offering;
and for the sacrifice of peace offerings two oxen, five rams, five male goats, five lambs sons of a year. This was the offering of Elizur son of Shedeur.
On the fifth day Shelumiel son of Zurishaddai, the leader of the sons of Simeon, presented.
His offering was one silver platter, one hundred thirty in weight; one silver bowl, seventy shekels according to the sanctuary shekel; both filled with fine flour mixed with oil as a grain offering;
one dish, ten, gold, filled with incense;
one bull, son of the herd, one ram, one lamb son of its year, for a burnt offering;
one male goat of the goats for a sin offering;
and for the sacrifice of peace offerings two oxen, five rams, five male goats, five lambs sons of a year. This was the offering of Shelumiel son of Zurishaddai.
On the sixth day Eliasaph son of Deuel, the leader of the sons of Gad, presented.
His offering was one silver platter, one hundred thirty in weight; one silver bowl, seventy shekels according to the sanctuary shekel; both filled with fine flour mixed with oil as a grain offering;
one dish, ten, gold, filled with incense;
one bull, son of the herd, one ram, one lamb son of its year, for a burnt offering;
one male goat of the goats for a sin offering;
and for the sacrifice of peace offerings two oxen, five rams, five male goats, five lambs sons of a year. This was the offering of Eliasaph son of Deuel.
On the seventh day Elishama son of Ammihud, the leader of the sons of Ephraim, presented.
His offering was one silver platter, one hundred thirty in weight; one silver bowl, seventy shekels according to the sanctuary shekel; both filled with fine flour mixed with oil as a grain offering;
one dish, ten, gold, filled with incense;
one bull, son of the herd, one ram, one lamb son of its year, for a burnt offering;
one male goat of the goats for a sin offering;
and for the sacrifice of peace offerings two oxen, five rams, five male goats, five lambs sons of a year. This was the offering of Elishama son of Ammihud.
On the eighth day Gamaliel son of Pedahzur, the leader of the sons of Manasseh, presented.
His offering was one silver platter, one hundred thirty in weight; one silver bowl, seventy shekels according to the sanctuary shekel; both filled with fine flour mixed with oil as a grain offering;
one dish, ten, gold, filled with incense;
one bull, son of the herd, one ram, one lamb son of its year, for a burnt offering;
one male goat of the goats for a sin offering;
and for the sacrifice of peace offerings two oxen, five rams, five male goats, five lambs sons of a year. This was the offering of Gamaliel son of Pedahzur.
On the ninth day Abidan son of Gideoni, the leader of the sons of Benjamin, presented.
His offering was one silver platter, one hundred thirty in weight; one silver bowl, seventy shekels according to the sanctuary shekel; both filled with fine flour mixed with oil as a grain offering;
one dish, ten, gold, filled with incense;
one bull, son of the herd, one ram, one lamb son of its year, for a burnt offering;
one male goat of the goats for a sin offering;
and for the sacrifice of peace offerings two oxen, five rams, five male goats, five lambs sons of a year. This was the offering of Abidan son of Gideoni.
On the tenth day Ahiezer son of Ammishaddai, the leader of the sons of Dan, presented.
His offering was one silver platter, one hundred thirty in weight; one silver bowl, seventy shekels according to the sanctuary shekel; both filled with fine flour mixed with oil as a grain offering;
one dish, ten, gold, filled with incense;
one bull, son of the herd, one ram, one lamb son of its year, for a burnt offering;
one male goat of the goats for a sin offering;
and for the sacrifice of peace offerings two oxen, five rams, five male goats, five lambs sons of a year. This was the offering of Ahiezer son of Ammishaddai.
On the eleventh day Pagiel son of Ocran, the leader of the sons of Asher, presented.
His offering was one silver platter, one hundred thirty in weight; one silver bowl, seventy shekels according to the sanctuary shekel; both filled with fine flour mixed with oil as a grain offering;
one dish, ten, gold, filled with incense;
one bull, son of the herd, one ram, one lamb son of its year, for a burnt offering;
one male goat of the goats for a sin offering;
and for the sacrifice of peace offerings two oxen, five rams, five male goats, five lambs sons of a year. This was the offering of Pagiel son of Ocran.
On the twelfth day Ahira son of Enan, the leader of the sons of Naphtali, presented.
His offering was one silver platter, one hundred thirty in weight; one silver bowl, seventy shekels according to the sanctuary shekel; both filled with fine flour mixed with oil as a grain offering;
one dish, ten, gold, filled with incense;
one bull, son of the herd, one ram, one lamb son of its year, for a burnt offering;
one male goat of the goats for a sin offering;
and for the sacrifice of peace offerings two oxen, five rams, five male goats, five lambs sons of a year. This was the offering of Ahira son of Enan.
This was the dedication of the altar on the day of its anointing, from the leaders of Israel: twelve silver platters, twelve silver bowls, twelve gold dishes.
One hundred and thirty was each one silver platter, and seventy each one bowl; all the silver of the vessels was two thousand and four hundred, according to the sanctuary shekel.
Twelve gold dishes filled with incense, ten ten per dish according to the sanctuary shekel; all the gold of the dishes was twenty and a hundred.
All the herd for the burnt offering was twelve bulls, twelve rams, twelve lambs sons of a year, with their grain offering; and twelve male goats of the goats for a sin offering.
And all the herd of the sacrifice of peace offerings was twenty-four bulls, sixty rams, sixty male goats, sixty lambs sons of a year. This was the dedication of the altar after its anointing.

And when Moses entered the Tent of Meeting to speak with Him, he heard the voice speaking to him from above the mercy seat which was upon the ark of the Testimony, from between the two cherubim; and He spoke to him.
